name: Tikal
description: Ancient Mayan city and UNESCO World Heritage Site. One of the largest archaeological sites of pre-Columbian Maya civilization.

name: Lago de Atitlán
description: Stunning volcanic lake surrounded by three volcanoes and indigenous Mayan villages.

name: Antigua Guatemala
description: Former capital city, famous for its well-preserved Spanish colonial architecture and ruins.

name: Volcán de Agua
description: Stratovolcano near Antigua, popular for hiking with stunning views of the valley.

name: Semuc Champey
description: Natural limestone bridge with stepped turquoise pools, surrounded by lush jungle.

name: Quiriguá
description: Archaeological site known for its impressive Mayan stelae and zoomorphs. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Chichicastenango
description: Renowned for its vibrant Thursday and Sunday markets, one of the largest indigenous markets in Central America, rich in Mayan traditions.

name: Volcán Pacaya
description: One of Guatemala's most active volcanoes. Visitors can hike close to lava flows and walk on hardened lava fields near Guatemala City.

name: Río Dulce
description: A stunning river gorge connecting Lake Izabal to the Caribbean coast, flanked by dense jungle, howler monkeys, and hot springs.

name: Livingston
description: Unique Caribbean town accessible only by boat, home to the Garifuna community with their own language, music, and cuisine.

name: Flores
description: Charming colonial island town in Lake Petén Itzá, gateway to Tikal and other northern Mayan sites. Connected to the mainland by a causeway.

name: Laguna Lachuá
description: Nearly perfect circular lake hidden in a national park in northern Alta Verapaz, with crystal-clear turquoise water and pristine jungle.

name: Volcán Santiaguito
description: Active lava dome complex on the flank of Santa María volcano. One of the most continuously active volcanoes in the world, offering rare views of ongoing eruptions.

name: Quetzaltenango (Xela)
description: Guatemala's second largest city, nestled in a highland valley. Known for its neoclassical architecture, vibrant indigenous culture, and language schools.

name: Grutas de Lanquín
description: Impressive cave system near the town of Lanquín in Alta Verapaz. At dusk, thousands of bats emerge from the cave in a spectacular natural show.

name: El Mirador
description: Remote mega-site in the northern Petén jungle, home to La Danta — one of the largest pyramids by volume ever built. Accessible only by a multi-day hike.

name: Monterrico
description: Pacific black-sand beach and nature reserve, famous for sea turtle nesting season and its mangrove canal system.

name: Volcán Tajumulco
description: At 4,220 m, the highest peak in Central America. A challenging two-day climb rewarded with panoramic views of Guatemala and Mexico.
